items.body:
🔴 **Design Issue** — `AzureSupportedClouds` uses a closed template literal type

```ts
export type AzureSupportedClouds = `${AzureClouds}`;
```

This produces a closed union of only the three literal strings (`"AZURE_CHINA_CLOUD" | "AZURE_PUBLIC_CLOUD" | "AZURE_US_GOVERNMENT"`), which prevents callers from passing any other string value. For extensible service-defined enums, the type should be `string` so the SDK remains forward-compatible with new enum values added by the service.

**Fix:** Change the TypeSpec definition to emit `AzureSupportedClouds` as `string` (or a `string`-extensible union) rather than a template-literal type derived from the closed enum. File an issue in the [generation tool repository](https://github.com/Azure/autorest.typescript/issues) if this is a codegen regression.

🔴 **Design Issue** — `AzureClouds` enum uses `UPPER_SNAKE_CASE` member names

```ts
export enum AzureClouds {
    AZURE_CHINA_CLOUD = "AZURE_CHINA_CLOUD",
    AZURE_PUBLIC_CLOUD = "AZURE_PUBLIC_CLOUD",
    AZURE_US_GOVERNMENT = "AZURE_US_GOVERNMENT"
}
```

Per Azure SDK TypeScript guidelines, enum members must use **PascalCase**. The current names (`AZURE_CHINA_CLOUD`, `AZURE_PUBLIC_CLOUD`, `AZURE_US_GOVERNMENT`) violate this convention and are inconsistent with all other enums in this file.

Additionally, the enum is not prefixed with `Known` (as all extensible enums in this package are), and there is no corresponding `KnownAzureClouds` counterpart.

**Fix:** Add `@clientName` decorators in the TypeSpec spec to rename enum members to PascalCase (e.g., `AzureChinaCloud`, `AzurePublicCloud`, `AzureUsGovernment`) and file an issue in the [generation tool repository](https://github.com/Azure/autorest.typescript/issues) if this is a codegen bug.

2 API design issues found in `@azure/arm-azurestackhci` v4.0.0 (stable, first TypeSpec migration). Both are recurring patterns seen across multiple management SDK PRs: (1) the `AzureClouds` enum uses `UPPER_SNAKE_CASE` member names instead of the required PascalCase, and (2) `AzureSupportedClouds` is typed as a closed template literal type `\`${AzureClouds}\`` instead of `string`, preventing forward-compatibility with new service-defined values. No tool issues (CHANGELOG compares against 4.0.0-beta.2, not an alpha; no `apiRefLink` with incorrect `?view=azure-node-preview`).

<details>
<summary>📊 Structured Report</summary>

```json
{"agent":"mgmt-reviewer","pr":37230,"summary":"issues_found","findings":[{"file":"sdk/azurestackhci/arm-azurestackhci/review/arm-azurestackhci-node.api.md","line":190,"issueType":"design","category":"naming","description":"AzureClouds enum members use UPPER_SNAKE_CASE (AZURE_CHINA_CLOUD, AZURE_PUBLIC_CLOUD, AZURE_US_GOVERNMENT) instead of PascalCase. Missing Known* prefix."},{"file":"sdk/azurestackhci/arm-azurestackhci/review/arm-azurestackhci-node.api.md","line":224,"issueType":"design","category":"type-safety","description":"AzureSupportedClouds uses closed template literal type `${AzureClouds}` instead of string, breaking forward extensibility."}]}
```

</details>
